description: Comprehensive National League season-by-season data 1876-2025 with key seasons highlighted and era annotations
source: Compiled from Baseball-Reference.com/leagues/NL, Baseball Almanac, and Baseball Data Hub

dead_ball_early.notes: NL founded with 8 teams; expanded to 12 in 1900; varied schedules from 60-112 games

dead_ball.notes: Standard 154-game era begins; NY Giants and Boston/Chicago teams dominant

power_transition.notes: St. Louis Cardinals dynasty begins (5 pennants in 1920s); Cubs, Giants, Brooklyn competitive

postwar_modern.notes: Cardinals dynasty 1940s-60s (9 pennants); Dodgers/Giants rivalry; franchise relocation 1958

expansion.notes: 162-game schedule begins 1962; NL expands to 16 teams 1969; Miracle Mets, Big Red Machine

rivalries_modern.notes: Braves dynasty 14 straight division titles 1991-2005; 1994 strike cancels season

contemporary.notes: Cardinals, Giants, D-backs cycling pennants; Cubs drought ends 2016; Dodgers emergence

recent.notes: Modern Dodgers dynasty; 6 pennants 2016-2025; 8 straight NL West 2013-2020 and 2022-2025; 9th Dodgers WS title 2025

key_seasons.notes: 108-win Mets; Mookie Wilson error/Ray Knight heroics
COVID 60-game season; most dominant Dodgers ever
Back-to-back WS titles; 9th Dodgers WS title
